title: Consumer Preferences and Behavioural & Non-Behavioural: A Microeconomics Analysis
abstract: Examined evolving consumer preferences in digital media consumption, focusing on short-form video content, hook strategies, and viewer engagement patterns through survey-based analysis.
methodology: Primary survey data collection, micro-econometric modeling
results: Found significant correlation between hook strategies and viewer retention

title: Impact of Video Engagement Metrics on Viewer Satisfaction: A Micro-Econometric Analysis Based on Primary Survey Data
abstract: Investigated how engagement metrics (likes, comments, shares, watch time) influence viewer satisfaction using micro-econometric techniques.
dataset: Primary survey data and platform analytics
results: Watch time and comment sentiment are the strongest predictors

title: Interest Rates and Corporate Investment in India (2012–2024)
abstract: Investigated the relationship between prevailing interest rates and corporate investment decisions in India using econometric tools including OLS regression and time-series data.
results: Inverse relationship between interest rates and investment, with lag effects
